sessions.intent: Move template stamping from a post-parse walk onto the read: the reader hands the type its template mode at construction, the type decides (it owns its holes). Trust rides the reader, not the content.

before.assumptions: ReadContext is the natural mode carrier (IReader work landed). text ctor's canTemplate:true hardcoded "plang" mode-blind — the bug. Goal is the only inherently-authored read.
before.risk: Security-critical: the mode must NOT ride the shared _inbound Wire (also reads runtime messages). FromWire rebuilds from already-parsed values — its seam can't delete blind. Two goal-load paths (goal.list via plang serializer, GoalCall via catalog options).

actions.context: ReadContext gains Template (string?). text ctor: bool canTemplate -> string? template; Template = (template != null && HasHoles) ? template : null. The type owns the holes-decision so HasVariableReference stays correct.
text.Read + text/Default.Read pass ctx.Template (mode-gated). path keeps "plang" (no read-mode at its ctor; mode-gating path deferred, behavior preserved).

Wire gains a Template ctor param, feeds it into the ReadContext for ITypeReader.Read. Plang serializer gains a dedicated _authored options (template="plang"); Deserialize<goal> routes to it (goal is the inherently-authored type). The single trusted construction site.
Security test: same %ref% bytes stamp under authored mode, stay literal under runtime mode, holeless never stamps. Updated 5 DataTests canTemplate:true -> "plang".

after.status: Increment 1 landed — born-with-template on the read path, mode-gated, proven. Seams still in place (idempotent); StampedForm removal + path-2 read-stamp + container slots + FromWire are later increments.
after.health: green — dev.sh full all 6 projects green (counts identical to baseline; one flaky Types failure cleared on re-run). Security test passes. Runtime template-rendering suite (789) green.
after.notes: The mode rides _authored only; _inbound (runtime messages) stays mode-off. GoalCall path 2 (catalog options) still seam-stamps until its read is routed through _authored too.
